text: L-Isoleucine, chemical formula C6H13NO2, molecular weight 131.17, is a pure organic molecular crystal without solvent or hydrate. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar monoclinic space group P21 (No. 4), exhibiting piezoelectric properties. The crystal structure was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction (CCDC 1207696, CSD refcode LISLEU). This compound was studied as part of a systematic investigation into amino acid piezoelectricity using modified Stranski-Krastanov growth via physical vapor deposition. L-Isoleucine crystals self-assembled into vertical ordered sheet arrays with high crystallinity. Experimental piezoelectric measurements via piezoresponse force microscopy confirmed piezoelectric activity. The ordered array structures demonstrated enhanced piezoelectric performance compared to powder crystals. The work revealed that L-isoleucine, along with L-valine and L-leucine, could be fabricated into high-performance piezoelectric nanogenerators with significantly enhanced output voltage compared to random powder-based devices, making them promising candidates for energy harvesting and sensing applications.
property_ref_title: Modified Stranski-Krastanov Growth of Amino Acid Arrays toward Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting
